name: agent-architect-tools
description: Single-package VS Code extension for Agent Architect session inspection, live chat interop, and MCP tooling, with an internal CLI harness for scripts and tests.

agentArchitectTools.showSessionsView.description: Show the Agent Sessions view in the Explorer.

agentArchitectTools.sessionDiscoveryScop.description: Choose whether the Explorer session view and workspace commands use only the current workspace's session storage or all local workspaceStorage roots.

agentArchitectTools.postCreateTimeoutMs.description: Timeout in milliseconds to wait for a persisted session mutation after dispatch (used by focused-send and createChat workflows).

agentArchitectTools.waitForPersistedDefa.description: When true, focused-send calls will wait for a persisted session mutation by default. Set to false to make focused-send non-blocking by default. Individual calls can override with the request flag `waitForPersisted`.

languageModelTools.name: list_agent_sessions
languageModelTools.userDescription: List recent stored local chat sessions from workspace storage.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List recent stored local chat sessions. Use this to discover available session ids before inspecting a specific session. The result is bounded text with session id, timestamp, size, and path. This tool is read-only and only inspects persisted local metadata.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return.

languageModelTools.name: get_agent_session_index
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a bounded trailing index of recent persisted rows for one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a bounded trailing index of recent persisted rows for one stored local chat session. Use this to inspect recent activity without exporting the full session. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

languageModelTools.name: get_agent_session_window
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a bounded window of persisted rows around matching anchor text in one session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a bounded window of persisted rows around matching anchor text in one stored local chat session. Use this when you need local context around a phrase or event rather than a full export. You may also anchor the window at the latest persisted compaction boundary by setting afterLatestCompact=true. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

anchorText.description: Text to search for in persisted rows.

anchorOccurrence.description: When multiple anchor matches exist, choose the first or last match after any compaction filter.

afterLatestCompact.description: Restrict the result to rows or blocks at or after the latest persisted compaction boundary.

before.description: Number of rows before each match to include.

after.description: Number of rows after each match to include.

maxMatches.description: Maximum number of matching windows to include.

includeNoise.description: Include low-signal rows such as selections and model-state rows.

languageModelTools.name: export_agent_session_markdown
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a markdown export of one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a markdown export of one stored local chat session. Use this when you need a broader persisted-session view than an index, snapshot, or profile. This extension surface is read-only and returns markdown inline instead of writing files.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

includeNoise.description: Include low-signal rows such as selections and model-state rows.

languageModelTools.name: export_agent_evidence_transcript
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a canonical evidence transcript for one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a canonical evidence transcript from persisted transcript artifacts for one stored local chat session. Use this when you need a findings-safe transcript view with explicit omissions and provenance. By default this agent-facing tool returns a compact block inventory; pass detailLevel=full when you need verbatim payload contents. You can additionally filter from the latest persisted compaction boundary, from an anchor block, or cap the emitted block count. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

anchorText.description: Optional text to search for in evidence blocks after any compaction filter.

anchorOccurrence.description: When multiple anchor matches exist, choose the first or last match after any compaction filter.

afterLatestCompact.description: Restrict the result to rows or blocks at or after the latest persisted compaction boundary.

maxBlocks.description: Maximum number of evidence blocks to emit after the applied filters.

detailLevel.description: Control how much inline detail is returned. Use summary for compact status or inventory output, or full for raw evidence sections and complete listings.

languageModelTools.name: get_agent_session_snapshot
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a compact current-state snapshot for one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a compact current-state snapshot for one stored local chat session. Use this for a small current-state summary derived from persisted artifacts. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

includeNoise.description: Include low-signal rows such as selections and model-state rows.

languageModelTools.name: estimate_agent_context_breakdown
languageModelTools.userDescription: Estimate persisted context pressure by category for one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Estimate persisted context pressure by category for one stored local chat session. Use this when you need a bounded heuristic picture of context utilization from persisted artifacts. By default this agent-facing tool returns a compact summary; pass detailLevel=full for the complete breakdown and signal list. You can restrict the estimate to content after the latest persisted compaction boundary and optionally cap the number of latest request families included. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

includeNoise.description: Include low-signal rows such as selections and model-state rows.

afterLatestCompact.description: Restrict the result to rows or blocks at or after the latest persisted compaction boundary.

latestRequestFamilies.description: Optional cap on how many latest request families to include in the bounded estimate.

detailLevel.description: Control how much inline detail is returned. Use summary for compact status or inventory output, or full for raw evidence sections and complete listings.

languageModelTools.name: get_agent_session_profile
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a findings-first diagnostic profile for one stored session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a findings-first diagnostic profile for one stored local chat session. Use this when you want the main risks and context signals summarized from persisted artifacts. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

sessionFile.description: Absolute path to a chatSessions/<sessionId>.jsonl file.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified discovered session.

includeNoise.description: Include low-signal rows such as selections and model-state rows.

languageModelTools.name: survey_agent_sessions
languageModelTools.userDescription: Compare several recent stored sessions with compact diagnostic metrics.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Compare several recent stored local chat sessions with compact diagnostic metrics. Use this when you need a quick cross-session view instead of inspecting only one session. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

storageRoots.description: Optional absolute workspaceStorage roots to scan instead of the defaults.

scope.description: Discovery scope. Use current-workspace to filter to the active VS Code workspace when available, or all-local to scan all local workspaceStorage roots.

limit.description: Maximum number of recent sessions to include in the survey.

languageModelTools.name: list_copilot_cli_sessions
languageModelTools.userDescription: List recent Copilot CLI session-state directories.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List recent Copilot CLI session-state directories from ~/.copilot/session-state or a supplied root. Use this before inspecting a specific Copilot CLI worker lane. This tool is read-only and returns a bounded text list.

sessionStateRoot.description: Optional absolute root for Copilot CLI session-state instead of ~/.copilot/session-state.

limit.description: Maximum number of Copilot CLI sessions to return.

languageModelTools.name: inspect_copilot_cli_session
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a bounded inspection of one Copilot CLI session-state entry.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a bounded inspection of one Copilot CLI session-state entry, including the latest persisted turn summary from events.jsonl. Use this when you need the latest worker-lane outcome without reading the raw log. This tool is read-only and returns markdown.

sessionStateRoot.description: Optional absolute root for Copilot CLI session-state instead of ~/.copilot/session-state.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix Copilot CLI session identifier. If omitted, the latest session is used.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified Copilot CLI session-state entry.

languageModelTools.name: send_prompt_to_copilot_cli_session
languageModelTools.displayName: Send Prompt To Copilot CLI Session
languageModelTools.userDescription: Send a prompt to an exact Copilot CLI session-state resource.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Send a prompt to an exact Copilot CLI session-state resource using the Copilot CLI-specific openSessionWithPrompt command. Use this when Local chat cannot target an exact session reliably and you need a verifiable worker-lane transport instead. This tool affects the VS Code UI and targets Copilot CLI sessions, not ordinary Local chats.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix Copilot CLI session identifier. If omitted, the latest session is used unless latest is false.

latest.description: Use the most recently modified Copilot CLI session-state entry. Defaults to true when sessionId is omitted.

prompt.description: The prompt to send into the target Copilot CLI session.

languageModelTools.name: inspect_live_chat_support
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render the current Copilot Chat live-interaction support matrix for this build.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render the current Copilot Chat live-interaction support matrix for this build. Use this when you need an explicit, evidence-bound answer about whether Local live chat can open new chats, set mode/model, select a custom agent, or target an exact existing session. By default this agent-facing tool returns a compact capability summary; pass detailLevel=full for runtime command and manifest sections. The tool is read-only and combines current runtime commands with the installed Copilot Chat manifest.

detailLevel.description: Control how much inline detail is returned. Use summary for compact status or inventory output, or full for raw evidence sections and complete listings.

languageModelTools.name: inspect_runtime_chat_commands
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render a bounded inventory of runtime and manifest chat/session commands.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a bounded inventory of currently registered runtime chat/session commands plus related manifest-declared Copilot Chat commands. Use this when you need to exhaust possible Local reopen or resume workarounds before concluding that exact Local reopen is unsupported. By default this agent-facing tool returns a compact summary with candidate counts; pass detailLevel=full for complete command lists.

detailLevel.description: Control how much inline detail is returned. Use summary for compact status or inventory output, or full for raw evidence sections and complete listings.

languageModelTools.name: probe_local_reopen_candidates
languageModelTools.userDescription: Try runtime reopen candidates against a specific Local chat session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Try runtime chat reopen candidates against a specific Local chat session and verify whether the requested editor-hosted Local chat actually opens in the current window. Use this when exact Local reopen appears unsupported but runtime command inventory still shows possible openSessionIn... or showAsChatSession workarounds.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix Local session identifier to probe for a reopen workaround.

languageModelTools.name: inspect_chat_focus_targets
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render the current chat focus target report from VS Code tab groups.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render the current chat focus target report from VS Code tab groups plus persisted live-chat titles. Use this before editor-focused live-chat send when you need evidence about which visible tab currently looks like the intended editor-hosted Local chat.

languageModelTools.name: inspect_chat_focus_debug
languageModelTools.userDescription: Render raw debug details for current editor chat tabs.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Render a raw debug report for current editor chat tabs, including shallow object keys and collected string hints from tab input metadata. Use this when a Local editor chat appears visible in the UI but cannot be matched to a persisted session title through the normal focus-target report. By default this agent-facing tool returns a compact likely-chat-editor summary; pass detailLevel=full for raw object keys and string hints for every tab.

detailLevel.description: Control how much inline detail is returned. Use summary for compact status or inventory output, or full for raw evidence sections and complete listings.

languageModelTools.name: list_live_agent_chats
languageModelTools.userDescription: List local live chat sessions that can be reopened or continued.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List local live chat sessions that can be reopened or continued. Use this before sending a new message into an existing chat. The tool is observational and reflects best-effort disk-backed session discovery.

limit.description: Maximum number of live chats to include.

languageModelTools.name: create_live_agent_chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Low-level new-chat create route. Prefer Send Message With Lifecycle when agent or model stability matters.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Low-level Local create route. Opens a new chat and sends the first prompt. Use this when you specifically want raw create behavior. Prefer send_message_with_lifecycle when requested agent or model must survive host inheritance or when persisted follow-up evidence matters. This tool affects the VS Code UI and rejects concurrent live-chat operations.

prompt.description: The first prompt to send in the new live chat.

agentName.description: Optional custom agent selector to prefix as #agentName in the dispatched prompt.

mode.description: Optional chat mode, for example Agent.

modelId.description: Optional model identifier to select for the live chat.

modelVendor.description: Optional model vendor for the live chat model selector.

partialQuery.description: Open with a partial query instead of dispatching a full request when supported.

blockOnResponse.description: Block until a response is produced when supported by the underlying chat command.

requireSelectionEvidence.description: Fail if the requested agent, mode, or model selection cannot be explicitly evidenced after dispatch.

languageModelTools.name: close_visible_live_chat_tabs
languageModelTools.userDescription: Close visible editor-hosted live chat tabs for one exact session.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Close visible editor-hosted live chat tabs for an exact or prefix session identifier without deleting the persisted session itself. Use this for safe Local cleanup after probes or tests when the target chat may still be open in editor tabs. This tool affects the VS Code UI and uses the same exact-session self-targeting guard as other live-chat mutation routes.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix session identifier for the live chat whose visible editor tabs should be closed.

languageModelTools.name: send_message_with_lifecycle
languageModelTools.userDescription: Preferred high-level Local create route when agent or model stability matters.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Preferred high-level Local create route. Seeds a new chat, patches persisted mode or model when needed, and then continues through the normal Local follow-up path. Use this instead of raw create when requested agent or model may be overridden by host state. This tool still depends on the same Local follow-up surface and cannot bypass host limits such as missing ordinary Local exact-session send.

prompt.description: The real prompt to send after the lifecycle has stabilized the target chat.

agentName.description: Optional requested custom agent name. The lifecycle will resolve a workspace agent file when possible and patch persisted mode before the real prompt is sent.

mode.description: Optional explicit mode id to patch into persisted session state before the real prompt is sent.

modelId.description: Optional model identifier to request during create and patch from persisted metadata if the create path falls back.

modelVendor.description: Optional model vendor for the requested model selector.

blockOnResponse.description: Block until the real follow-up response is produced when supported by the underlying lifecycle path.

requireSelectionEvidence.description: Fail if the final lifecycle result cannot explicitly evidence the requested selection.

languageModelTools.name: send_message_to_live_agent_chat
languageModelTools.displayName: Send Message To Live Agent Chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Advanced exact-session continuation for an existing live chat.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Advanced exact-session continuation by session id. Use this only when you already have the target session and need to probe exact-session behavior. On builds where ordinary Local exact send is unsupported, this tool may still fall back to reveal plus focused submit and therefore remains subject to host limits.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix session identifier for the live chat to continue.

prompt.description: The prompt to send into the existing live chat.

agentName.description: Optional custom agent selector to prefix as #agentName in the dispatched prompt.

mode.description: Optional chat mode override, for example Agent.

modelId.description: Optional model identifier to select for the live chat.

modelVendor.description: Optional model vendor for the live chat model selector.

partialQuery.description: Open with a partial query instead of dispatching a full request when supported.

blockOnResponse.description: Block until a response is produced when supported by the underlying chat command.

requireSelectionEvidence.description: Fail if the requested agent, mode, or model selection cannot be explicitly evidenced after dispatch.

allowTransportWorkaround.description: If true, and this build lacks ordinary Local exact send, allow a decoy-session workaround when the latest-session self-targeting guard would otherwise block the send. This is transport-workaround evidence, not strict exact-session proof.

languageModelTools.name: send_message_to_focused_live_chat
languageModelTools.displayName: Send Message To Focused Live Chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Send a prompt through the currently focused live chat input.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Send a prompt through the currently focused live chat input using focusInput plus chat.submit when this build exposes those commands. Use this only when the intended Local chat is already visible and focused, because it is a best-effort focused-thread transport rather than an exact session-targeted API. This tool affects the VS Code UI and rejects concurrent live-chat operations. A heuristic self-targeting guard blocks use when the current invoking conversation appears to be the focused thread.

prompt.description: The prompt to send through the currently focused live chat input.

agentName.description: Optional custom agent selector to prefix as #agentName in the dispatched prompt.

mode.description: Optional chat mode override. Focused live send cannot reliably apply or prove this on the current build.

modelId.description: Optional model identifier. Focused live send cannot reliably apply or prove this on the current build.

modelVendor.description: Optional model vendor for the requested model selector.

partialQuery.description: Reserved for compatibility; focused live send uses a partial prefill before submit on this build.

blockOnResponse.description: Reserved for compatibility with other live chat mutation tools.

requireSelectionEvidence.description: Fail if the requested agent selection cannot be explicitly evidenced after dispatch.

languageModelTools.name: focus_visible_editor_live_chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Steer focus to a visible editor-hosted live chat without sending a prompt.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Steer focus to the active editor group, or to a visible editor-hosted chat tab matching an optional live session id, and verify that the resulting active tab still looks like an editor-hosted Local chat. Use this to satisfy the target-confirmation gate before any focused editor-chat prompt send. This tool affects the VS Code UI but does not dispatch a prompt.

sessionId.description: Optional exact or prefix session identifier for the visible editor-hosted live chat to focus. When omitted, the active editor-group candidate is used.

languageModelTools.name: send_message_to_focused_editor_chat
languageModelTools.displayName: Send Message To Focused Editor Chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Steer focus to the active editor chat and send a prompt there.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Steer focus to the active editor group, or to a visible editor-hosted chat tab matching an optional live session id, verify that the resulting active tab still looks like an editor-hosted Local chat, and then dispatch a prompt through the focused chat input. Use this when a side-panel chat currently has focus but the intended Local target is already open in an editor tab. This tool affects the VS Code UI and remains best-effort until the touched session is verified from persisted artifacts. A heuristic self-targeting guard blocks ambiguous sends and prefers an explicit sessionId.

sessionId.description: Optional exact or prefix session identifier for the visible editor-hosted live chat to focus before submit. When omitted, the active editor-group candidate is used.

prompt.description: The prompt to send through the focused editor-hosted live chat.

agentName.description: Optional custom agent selector to prefix as #agentName in the dispatched prompt.

mode.description: Optional chat mode override. Focused editor send cannot reliably apply or prove this on the current build.

modelId.description: Optional model identifier. Focused editor send cannot reliably apply or prove this on the current build.

modelVendor.description: Optional model vendor for the requested model selector.

partialQuery.description: Reserved for compatibility; focused editor send uses a partial prefill before submit on this build.

blockOnResponse.description: Reserved for compatibility with other live chat mutation tools.

requireSelectionEvidence.description: Fail if the requested agent selection cannot be explicitly evidenced after dispatch.

languageModelTools.name: reveal_live_agent_chat
languageModelTools.userDescription: Re-open an existing live chat so the visible thread reloads current persisted state.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Re-open an existing live chat session in the side panel when the current build supports internal session-targeted chat commands. When a matching editor-hosted Local chat tab is already open, this path closes that tab first so the reopened session reloads current persisted state. Use this before inspection or best-effort follow-up when the visible thread must match persisted state.

sessionId.description: Exact or prefix session identifier for the live chat to reveal.

scripts.test: npm run build && node scripts/ensure-vscode-test-stub.mjs && node tests/test.mjs
